summary:
Given a grid of letters and a list of target words, find every target word that can be spelled out by walking through adjacent cells on the board. From any cell you may move up, down, left, or right to a neighboring cell, and a single cell cannot be reused within the same word.

Return the list of words from the input that can be formed this way. The order of the returned words does not matter. A trie built from the word list makes this efficient: it lets a single depth-first traversal of the board prune paths that cannot lead to any word.

examples.explanation: "oath" and "eat" trace valid paths of adjacent cells, while "pea" and "rain" cannot be formed on this board.
"abcb" would require revisiting the cell holding 'b', which is not allowed, so no word can be formed.

constraints: board[i][j] is a lowercase English letter
1 <= words[i].length <= 10 and words[i] consists of lowercase English letters
All strings in words are unique
